question.text: Under SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, what is the threshold of voting rights that triggers a mandatory open offer for an IGP (Institutionalized Group of Persons) listed company?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For IGP listed companies, SEBI SAST Regulations 2011 set the threshold at 49% of voting rights (instead of the standard 25%) before a mandatory open offer is triggered. This higher threshold acknowledges the unique governance structure of IGP entities. **The Trap:** 25% is the standard threshold for regular listed companies — candidates often default to this number without considering the IGP exception. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) touches on corporate governance concepts that interact with takeover thresholds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check the entity type of the target company before advising on acquisition thresholds — IGP companies have materially different trigger points that can change the entire deal structure.
